title: Gritty Mexican Pepper Pudding
description: A chewy, nutty dessert that blends warm chili spice with smooth horchata‑like texture, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: In a saucepan, bring oat milk to a gentle simmer with the cinnamon stick.
Stir in corn grits and reduce heat to low, cooking for 15 minutes until thickened.
Remove the cinnamon stick and whisk in sugar until dissolved.
Add ground cinnamon and chili powder, stirring until the mixture is aromatic and evenly spiced.
Turn off the heat and fold in cacao powder and vanilla extract for a smooth flavor.
Let the pudding cool slightly, then stir in toasted almond flakes for texture.
Transfer the pudding to a bowl, allowing it to set for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve warm or cold, garnished with extra almond flakes if desired.
Enjoy this spicy, nutty horchata‑style pudding as a midnight treat.
